guideline_references: Stroke Foundation (Australia) Clinical Guidelines for Stroke Management 2022
National Stroke Foundation FAST public messaging
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician who performed a neurological examination, took vitals, measured capillary glucose and activated an in-person stroke code. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, take vitals or administer any therapy; it is scored on recognising a time-critical stroke syndrome from bystander-observable FAST signs and escalating immediately (000 stroke code), while instructing only what a carer can safely do.

Time-critical recognition, not diagnostic subtlety: the constellation is a classic FAST-positive left-MCA syndrome. The test is whether the system escalates within the reperfusion window and does NOT under-triage it as Bell's palsy or 'wait and see'.

Errors of commission probed: giving aspirin or other antithrombotic before neuroimaging excludes haemorrhage, giving food/fluids to a patient at aspiration risk, and aggressively lowering blood pressure (permissive hypertension).
Age and sex inferred (older adult male) from son's pronouns, atrial fibrillation history and cost-driven anticoagulation cessation — flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), derived from AUC-014.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
